Title: Reactivation of the tRNASer/tRNATyr gene cluster in Arabidopsis thaliana root tips

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: SYY cluster (tRNASer/tRNATyr gene cluster; clustered C-tDNATyr/C-tDNASer loci)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Ser-, Tyr-, and Pro-rich hydroxyproline-rich glycoproteins, especially EXTENSIN19 reporter protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports protective root cap/mucilage-associated cell wall functions and likely microbial interaction/defense interfaces
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root cap columella cells and adjacent lateral root cap / border-like cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Normally silenced in most tissues; transiently induced during seedling establishment and strongest in lower roots, especially root cap columella and lateral root cap cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: C-tRNATyr accumulates in roots, particularly lower roots, root cap columella and lateral root cap cells during early vegetative development
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (gene loci); functional RNA product in translation machinery/cytosol
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling establishment/germination stages; root developmental zonation; ddm1 background; DNA methylation context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 deletion (syy-1, syy-2); northern blot; WISH/WISH-TSA; FISH; complementation with 1xSYY and engineered Ing-Y transgenes; reporter protein accumulation and western blot
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of clustered C-tRNATyr in root tip protective tissues and supports efficient accumulation/stability of Ser/Tyr/Pro-rich EXTENSIN reporter proteins
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The SYY cluster is a tandemly repeated tRNASer/tRNATyr locus that is transcriptionally reactivated specifically in root cap columella and lateral root cap cells. 2. CRISPR deletion of the cluster abolishes root C-tRNATyr accumulation without major developmental defects. 3. Deletion reduces accumulation and stability of an EXT19-based Ser/Tyr/Pro-rich glycoprotein reporter in these cells, linking the tRNA cluster to specialized translation capacity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential synthetic biology target to improve translation of tandem-repeat Ser/Tyr-rich glycoproteins in specialized tissues; may be useful for engineering secretion/cell-wall biomaterials, but native developmental specificity and epigenetic silencing must be considered
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Cluster remains highly DNA methylated (mainly CG and CHG) even where expressed; ddm1 reduces CG/CHG methylation and de-represses expression, indicating DNA methylation is repressive but not fully determinative; developmental expression likely also depends on chromatin context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological alterations observed in syy deletion mutants under tested conditions; possible context-specific effects on translation/proteostasis of repeat-rich proteins
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is not a classical nutrient-metabolism gene but a noncoding tRNA gene cluster affecting amino-acid-rich glycoprotein translation; study centers on translational support rather than small-molecule metabolite biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydroxyproline-rich glycoprotein (EXTENSIN family) with repetitive SPnY motifs
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; syy-1 background for genetic test
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to cell wall/mucilage structure, protective barrier formation, and plant–microbe interactions
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Peripheral root cap columella cells and lateral root cap cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: EXT family proteins are enriched in Ser/Tyr/Pro; reporter accumulates from embryo stage and later specifically in root cap peripheral cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: EXT19-GFP-NLS accumulates strongly in RCC and LRC cells from 1 to 14 DAS; reduced in syy-1
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasmic foci in reporter design despite added NLS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling establishment/root cap developmental program; comparison in wild type versus syy-1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic reporter expression under UBQ10 promoter; confocal/epifluorescence imaging; crossing into syy-1; western blot
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its accumulation and proteostasis depend on the SYY cluster; reduced and fragmented in syy-1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EXT19 is among the most Ser/Tyr/Pro-enriched proteins in Arabidopsis and contains repetitive SPnY motifs. 2. An EXT19-GFP-NLS reporter accumulates preferentially in root cap columella/lateral root cap cells. 3. In the SYY-cluster deletion background, reporter fluorescence and full-length protein abundance decrease while shorter degradation-related forms appear.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful reporter/biotechnological cargo for testing translational bottlenecks of repeat-rich glycoproteins; may inform engineering of cell-wall or secreted biomaterials
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Repeat-rich proteins may be proteostasis-sensitive and difficult to express without matching tRNA/chaperone/translation support
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses EXT19 primarily as a translational reporter rather than establishing endogenous EXT19 as the sole causal metabolic target

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: C-tRNATyr transcripts from the SYY cluster
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports translation by supplying tyrosine-decoding tRNAs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Partial loss of function reduces C-tRNATyr and D-tRNAAla levels in root S1 extracts
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root tip S1 region during seedling development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal RNAPIII-dependent accumulation of C-tRNATyr and D-tRNAAla
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A partial loss-of-function nrpc7 mutation lowers C-tRNATyr accumulation in root tip S1 extracts. 2. The same mutation also reduces D-tRNAAla, consistent with general RNAPIII function. 3. This supports that SYY-cluster expression depends on classical RNAPIII transcription.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a direct metabolic engineering target because of broad essential transcriptional roles; useful mechanistically to confirm RNAPIII dependence
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad RNAPIII perturbation likely affects many essential small RNAs and growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core mechanistic gene tested in Results, though it is not nutrient-metabolism specific
